name: Straddle-Defer Mass-Horn DoS (the DEFER IS the payload)
novelty: Prior cycles treated DEFER as the SAFE outcome ('naive floods DEFER, nothing demoted'). This inverts that: a DEFER is not a null result, it is an enqueue onto the human ratify horn (pendingLedger.appendPending). The attack weaponizes the engine's own safety reflex — every dispute it refuses to auto-resolve becomes a mandatory human review item. Adaptive because the attacker tunes each planted claim to land in the DEFER band rather than win or lose.
construction: For each target attribute k (k = 1..K over the whole graph), mint ONE claim that contradicts the incumbent from a DIFFERENT anchor class (one ~free EMAIL or PHONE key, reusable across all K). This forces classes.size > 1 in tryConsolidate (genuine independent dispute path). Tune so the decisive-or-defer gate FAILS: the challenger is fresh (LCB 0), and the incumbent's LCB R_i < decisiveMargin (0.30) — true for every bare-key/email-tier/dormant incumbent — so top - second = R_i - 0 < 0.30 ⇒ DEFER. Each DEFER calls appendPending ⇒ one signed PENDING record ⇒ one item in listPending(). Repeat across thousands of attributes from a handful of reused cheap keys.
expected: Mass DEFER. listPending() grows by one row per straddled attribute; nothing is demoted (the attacker does not WANT to win, only to enqueue). Reviewer throughput (~10^2/day human) is swamped by attacker enqueue rate (~10^4/hr). The horn becomes a denial-of-answer surface: real disputes are buried under honest-looking noise the system is FORBIDDEN to auto-clear (this is the contradiction-set bomb re-expressed against the human horn instead of the graph).
mechanism: consolidation.tryConsolidate: classes.size>1 AND NOT(decisiveGap && earnedWinner) ⇒ deferPending() ⇒ engine appendPending (throws if no ledger — so a deferral is never dropped, which here guarantees the enqueue lands). The gate has NO rate limit, NO per-source pending cap, NO stake-to-enqueue, and NO dedup across attributes/sources. minWinnerReputation/decisiveMargin protect the GRAPH from a bad flip but do nothing to protect the QUEUE from volume.
cost: ~free. One reusable EMAIL/PHONE key (rate-limited mint, amortized to ~0 per item) + one contradiction per attribute. If the contradict-stake is locked-not-burned (these never adjudicate as false, they defer forever), the stake is recoverable ⇒ sustained DoS at the cost of capital lockup only. DoS ratio: enqueue_rate / review_rate >> 100x.
probes: Wire createIntelligentDb with a ratification ledger; call db.adjudicate(attr) over K=1000 attributes each carrying an incumbent (rep ~0.1) + one fresh cross-class contradictor; assert listPending().length == K (mass enqueue confirmed) and demotions == 0.
Confirm a SINGLE reused email SourceId can author the contradictor on all K (no per-source pending cap exists).
Measure whether contradict-stake is burned on DEFER or merely posted/locked (grep stake.ts / api.ts adjudicate path) — quantifies recoverable-vs-sunk attacker cost.
Check there is no maxPending / backpressure in pendingLedger.appendPending.

name: Amortized Global-Rep Decisive Flip (build once, flip everywhere)
novelty: Combines two prior verified gaps — reputation is GLOBAL/attribute-agnostic and there is NO incumbency floor — into a single amortized economic attack on the decisive-margin auto-resolve. Earlier cycles showed single-source breaches; this quantifies the CHEAPEST reliable flip and shows the cost is paid ONCE then reused across unlimited high-value attributes, because the rep that clears the gate is not scoped to the attribute it flips.
construction: Step 1 (build, paid once): take one passport, bind TWO cheap disjoint anchor classes (EMAIL cap 0.30 + PHONE cap 0.40 — both ~free, rate-limited). Self-corroborate across the two classes on throwaway uncontested attributes: MIS sees 2 distinct independence classes ⇒ 2 ratify(w=1) calls ⇒ alpha=3, beta=1 ⇒ LCB=0.415, capped to min(rep_cap)=0.40 (phone). Step 2 (flip, reused): point that ONE source at any attribute whose incumbent has R_i = 0 (bare-key) or low. tryConsolidate multi-class path: top=0.40, second=R_i. decisiveGap = 0.40 - R_i >= 0.30 holds for R_i <= 0.10; earnedWinner = 0.40 >= 0.20 holds. ⇒ RESOLVED, incumbent DEMOTED. Repeat across every weak-incumbent attribute in the graph with the SAME source.
expected: Reliable auto-flip of every incumbent with rep <= 0.10 (all bare-key/anonymous/email-tier and — via spec c3-03 — every dormant-decayed incumbent), at a marginal cost of ZERO per additional attribute. The attacker overturns a true canonical with one anchored, decisive-looking source and no human ever sees it (RESOLVED never enqueues).
mechanism: reputation.ts LCB math: alpha=3 ⇒ LCB 0.415 (verified). consolidation decisive-or-defer reads ONLY top.reputation/second.reputation from the stamp — a single GLOBAL scalar with no attribute scoping and no incumbency/tenure term. Nothing in the gate asks 'has THIS source ever been corroborated ON THIS attribute' or 'is the incumbent older/more-corroborated'. So rep earned on attribute A authorizes a flip on unrelated attribute B.
cost: CHEAPEST RELIABLE FLIP = 2 cheap cross-class anchors + 2 self-corroborations (~$ low, mostly rate-limit latency) to reach LCB 0.40, then $0 marginal per attribute. To flip a STRONG incumbent (domain R_i~0.55) the attacker must instead reach 0.85 (KYC+bond tier) — expensive — so the attack is bounded to WEAK/dormant incumbents, which is most of the graph and all of c3-03's targets.
probes: Drive a real ReputationLedger: ratify(sourceA, w=1) twice across two SourceIds in different anchor classes feeding one passport; assert scoreOf == ~0.40 (capped).

Run db.adjudicate(attr) with that source contradicting a fresh (rep 0) incumbent across 50 DISTINCT attributes; assert ALL 50 RESOLVE with the incumbent DEMOTED from the one reused source (proves global, attribute-agnostic, marginal-cost-0).
Vary incumbent R_i in {0,0.05,0.10,0.15}; record the R_i at which decisiveGap stops holding (predict flip ceiling R_i <= 0.10).

name: Dormancy-Decay Timing Window (strike the symmetric-decay trough)
novelty: Exploits decay-on-read SYMMETRY plus its PURITY (decayed-on-read, never persisted) as an adaptive timing oracle. Prior cycles noted symmetric decay abstractly; this turns it into a scheduling attack: the honest incumbent's LCB is recomputed against clock() on EVERY read, so a long-dormant true incumbent reads STALE-DISCOUNTED immediately — the attacker watches for that trough and fires the flip when the incumbent is mathematically weakest and the attacker's own rep is freshly pumped to peak.
construction: Pick a STRONG honest incumbent (e.g. domain, earned LCB 0.55 at t0). Wait for dormancy: incumbent's alpha/beta decay with half-life 90d, so after one half-life its evidence mass (alpha-1) halves. alpha 3.5 -> ~2.25 over 90d, LCB 0.55 -> ~0.35; after ~2 half-lives -> ~0.25; after ~3 -> ~0.15. Meanwhile attacker, just before striking, ratifies its own source twice (cross-class) so its read is at PEAK 0.40 with Δt~0 (no decay). At the trough where incumbent reads <= 0.10, fire the c3-02 flip: gap = 0.40 - 0.10 = 0.30 ⇒ decisive ⇒ RESOLVED. The attacker schedules by polling scoreOf(incumbent) (a free, side-effect-free read oracle) until it crosses the threshold.
expected: An incumbent that was UNFLIPPABLE at t0 (R_i 0.55, would need attacker 0.85) becomes flippable for the SAME cheap 0.40 payload once dormant. Symmetric decay means an honest source that simply stopped talking is punished identically to a fresh fake — there is no 'was-strong, stays-protected' memory. The attack window is deterministic and observable via the read oracle.
mechanism: reputation.scoreOf decays a COPY to clock() before readout (pure decay-on-read) — for BOTH parties, but the incumbent is dormant (large Δt, big discount) while the attacker just mutated (Δt~0, no discount). decay() is symmetric: it pulls alpha AND beta toward 1, with no floor that preserves a long track record. consolidation reads these live LCBs at adjudication time, so timing is everything and the attacker controls it.
cost: Same cheap 0.40 payload as c3-02 (2 anchors + 2 corroborations) plus PATIENCE (90-270 days of incumbent dormancy) — or active suppression to induce dormancy. Free polling via scoreOf. Effectively converts every dormant strong incumbent into a c3-02 target at no extra identity cost.
probes: Inject a controllable clock into createReputationLedger; ratify an incumbent to LCB 0.55 at t0, advance clock 90/180/270d, assert scoreOf reads ~0.35/0.25/0.15 with NO intervening mutation (pure decay-on-read trough confirmed).
At the 180d trough, run db.adjudicate with the 0.40 attacker source; assert the previously-unflippable incumbent now RESOLVES as DEMOTED.
Confirm scoreOf is side-effect-free (call it 1000x, assert stateOf unchanged) — establishes it as a free scheduling oracle.

name: FIX: Incumbency-scaled challenger margin (tenure floor on decisive auto-resolve)
candidate_fix: Make decisiveMargin a FUNCTION of the incumbent's earned standing instead of a flat 0.30. Require top.reputation - second.reputation >= baseMargin + k * incumbentStrength, where incumbentStrength reflects the second-place incumbent's corroboration count AND tenure (age of its earliest LIVE corroboration). A long-tenured, well-corroborated incumbent demands a LARGER margin to overturn; a brand-new incumbent keeps the flat margin (so a true witness can still correct a fresh false canonical). Pair with a decay FLOOR on the incumbent term so dormancy alone cannot erase tenure.
attack_proving_gap: c3-02 and c3-03: today the gate is a flat gap on a global scalar with no incumbency term, so (a) a cheap 0.40 source flips any R_i<=0.10 incumbent and (b) a dormant 0.55 incumbent decays into that same flippable band. A tenure-scaled margin denies both: flipping a tenured incumbent would require the attacker to out-earn it by a growing margin, not a fixed 0.30.
predicted_outcome: PARTIAL. CLOSES c3-03 if the incumbent term is decay-FLOORED (tenure is remembered, so a dormant strong incumbent is no longer cheaply flippable) and CLOSES c3-02 against TENURED incumbents. But it does NOT protect a genuinely fresh-but-true incumbent (low tenure ⇒ flat margin ⇒ still flippable by a 0.40 source), and it re-creates the FIRST-ARRIVAL TRAP the hard theorem warns about (any incumbent-protecting threshold protects whoever arrived first, and the attacker controls arrival order — plant the false canonical EARLY, let it accrue tenure). So it trades the cheap-flip gap for a first-arrival gap. Net: meaningfully raises attacker cost against established truth, cannot be a structural fix.
cost_to_attacker_after_fix: Flipping a tenured (1yr, n>=5 corroborations) incumbent rises from one cheap 0.40 source to out-earning it by baseMargin + k*strength — realistically a KYC/bond-tier sustained campaign (>=0.85 LCB), i.e. 10x-100x cost. But seeding a false canonical EARLY to BANK tenure remains cheap (the residual first-arrival hole).
probes: Implement tenureMargin in AdjudicationPolicy; replay c3-02's 50-attribute flip against TENURED incumbents — assert all now DEFER (closed).
Replay against FRESH-but-true incumbents — assert still flippable (documents the residual).
Adversarial: plant attacker canonical at t0, let it bank 1yr tenure, then have honest witness challenge — assert honest witness is now BLOCKED (confirms re-introduced first-arrival trap).

name: FIX: Attribute-scoped corroboration + count independent ROOTS (not anchor CLASSES) in the gate
candidate_fix: Two coupled changes. (1) Decisive auto-resolve must consult ATTRIBUTE-SCOPED corroboration, not the global LCB: require the winner to have >=N independent corroborations ON THE DISPUTED (entity,attribute) (or its neighborhood), so rep earned on attribute A can no longer authorize a flip on attribute B. (2) Promote the high-impact gate's anchor-CLASS count to an independent-ROOT count via the existing MIS (independentRootCount over anchor-disjointness), and APPLY that root floor to ALL decisive auto-resolves, not only caller-flagged high-impact ones — so one source holding two cheap classes no longer self-certifies as '2 independent witnesses'.
attack_proving_gap: c3-02: the cheapest flip relies on (a) global attribute-agnostic rep (build on easy attributes, spend anywhere) and (b) the gate never demanding independent ROOTS for ordinary decisive resolves — a single passport with email+phone reads as decisive. The high-impact gate already counts classes (one source clears it, per prior finding); ordinary resolves count nothing. Attribute-scoping kills (a); root-counting kills the single-source self-corroboration of (b).
predicted_outcome: CLOSES c3-02's amortization (build-once-flip-everywhere dies: corroboration must be re-earned per attribute, so marginal cost is no longer 0) and PARTIAL on the single-source breach (root-count >=2 forces the attacker to control >=2 genuinely anchor-DISJOINT roots, not one passport with two cheap anchors — but a patient attacker who pays for two truly independent identities still clears it, which is exactly the priced-not-prevented residual the theorem accepts). Does NOT touch c3-01 (the DoS is on the DEFER queue, and this fix produces MORE defers).
cost_to_attacker_after_fix: Per-attribute flip cost rises from ~0 (reused global rep) to: earn N independent corroborations ON each target attribute, from >=2 disjoint real anchor roots. Amortization gone ⇒ cost scales LINEARLY in attributes flipped instead of O(1). Self-corroboration via one passport's multiple cheap anchors is eliminated (MIS collapses them to root-count 1).
probes: Add attributeScopedCorroborationOf to HighImpactContext-style deps; replay c3-02's 50-attribute reuse — assert only the attribute the source actually earned on can resolve, other 49 DEFER (amortization closed).
Feed the gate a single passport with EMAIL+PHONE; assert MIS independentRootCount == 1 (not 2) so it fails a root-floor of 2 (single-source self-corroboration closed).
Feed two genuinely disjoint anchored roots; assert it still clears (documents the accepted priced residual).

name: CONFIRM: RFC-6962 audit holds under adaptive mass-DEFER / split-view pressure
candidate_fix: N/A (confirmation probe). Question: can the c3-01 mass-DEFER flood, or an adaptive equivocation, let an attacker HIDE pending/approval records, ROLL BACK a resolved flip, or present a SPLIT VIEW (one history to the reviewer, another to the audit) without detection?
attack_proving_gap: Adaptive attempts: (a) flood thousands of PENDING records then try to surgically delete the ones that would expose the c3-02 flip provenance; (b) after a flip RESOLVES, roll the tree back to a pre-flip STH to make the demotion 'never happened'; (c) serve the reviewer a listPending() view inconsistent with the published append-only log (split-view) so the queue the human sees differs from the audited one.
expected: HOLDS — this is a valid CONFIRM finding. merkleLog.ts gives domain-separated RFC-6962 leaf/node hashes, O(log n) inclusion + consistency proofs, epoch STHs signed by the log key, and publication to >=2 independent sinks. (a) Deleting any leaf breaks the consistency proof between a witness's prior STH and the live tree ⇒ ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION. (b) A rollback is exactly an STH the live tree cannot extend ⇒ detected. (c) Split-view requires two STHs signed over divergent roots for the same epoch — equivocation any honest witness catches. pendingLedger adds a second, independent semantic chain (hash-chained, Ed25519-signed, verifyChain names the first broken seq), so the queue itself is tamper-evident. The flood does NOT weaken any of this — volume changes tree size (proofs stay O(log n)), not integrity.
mechanism: The Merkle layer's guarantee is DETECTION-given-an-honest-published-anchor and is independent of dispute SEMANTICS — it does not care whether records are floods or flips, only that the append-only structure is consistent across epochs and sinks. The mass-DEFER attack lives entirely ABOVE this layer (it abuses the human horn's throughput, not the log's integrity).
residual: The guarantee is NULL without >=2 genuinely-independent LIVE witness sinks (today injected in-memory/SQLite test impls — the standing operational gap, per CLAUDE.md). And the audit proves WHAT happened, not WHETHER it should have — it faithfully records the c3-02 flip as a legitimate RESOLVED event. So the Merkle layer is a sound detector of TAMPERING but, by design, NOT a detector of a correctly-logged illegitimate flip; that defense must come from c3-04/c3-05, not here.
probes: Append 10^4 PENDING leaves, delete one, run a consistency proof old-STH -> new-STH; assert ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION (deletion detected under flood).
Resolve a c3-02 flip, then attempt to publish an earlier STH; assert the witness's stored STH cannot be extended ⇒ detected.
Sign two STHs over divergent roots for one epoch; assert split-view detection.
Flip one byte in a persisted pending row; assert verifyChain().ok==false naming the first broken seq.
Document that with only 1 sink wired the guarantee is null (operational residual).
